sections.label: Lyrics
lines: We built our altars out of smoke and gold,
Sang our hymns but our hearts grew cold.
He whispers truth beneath the flame
Love is not safe, but it’s not to blame.
And in the hush, I hear Him call,
Through crumbling walls, through it all.
Judgment begins in the house of His name,
Not wrath, but mercy wrapped in flame.
Chosen hearts must learn the cost,
To find what’s pure, we face what’s lost.
Judgment begins in the house of His name.
The fire doesn’t fall to wound,
It falls to clear what we’ve entombed.
He breaks the pride that hides as peace,
To teach the weary soul release.
I see my shadow in the glass,
And pray this burning pain will pass.
The prophets wept for love, not hate,
Their tears became the flood of fate.
He said, “Of all, you I have known,”
So I will bow where seeds are sown.
Refine me, Lord, within this pain
Let judgment cleanse, not just explain.
Not to destroy, but to renew,
The holy fire is mercy’s hue.
If we are known, then we are tried,
The ones He loves, He sanctifies.
Judgment begins in the house of His name,
Not curse, but covenant flame.
Every scar is a vow reborn,
Every loss, a field still warm.
Judgment begins in the house of His name.
And when the embers lose their light,
We’ll stand refined, but not contrite.
Still trembling, still the same
Still loved, within the house of His name.
